element_introduction_order.element: Individual unit types with distinct strengths/weaknesses
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn role differentiation (archers, berserks, dwarves, etc.)

element_introduction_order.element: Multi-unit group selection and tactical coordination
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Band-selection, shift-click, and keyboard group assignment
Predetermined reinforcement points introduce tactical timing
Resource scarcity creates tension; unit preservation becomes critical
Coordinating multiple unit types against varied enemy compositions

element_introduction_order.element: Balor and Fallen Lords narrative encounters
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Story climax requiring mastery of all previous mechanics

typical_tension_triggers: Multiple simultaneous enemy groups requiring divided attention
High difficulty curve criticisms suggest escalating enemy pressure
Loss of key units without replacement options
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: High difficulty campaign with escalating enemy pressure; criticized for being too demanding even on lowest settings, suggesting intense sustained tension rather than cyclical pulses

difficulty_curve.early_game: Controlled introduction of mechanics with safe, tutorial-like encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate complexity with reinforcement scheduling and multi-unit coordination
difficulty_curve.late_game: High difficulty with complex encounters against multiple enemy types and advanced Fallen Lords
notable_spikes: Campaign difficulty universally criticized as too high throughout, suggesting consistent steep challenge rather than isolated spikes
Late-game encounters with Balor and Fallen Lords likely represent culminating difficulty peaks

notable_level_patterns.description: Missions feature timed reinforcement arrival points, creating tactical decision windows around when and where to position forces for incoming support
Level design requires simultaneous management of diverse unit types (archers, berserks, dwarves) with different strengths, forcing players to coordinate complementary forces
Campaign-wide unit attrition with no recruitment creates escalating resource pressure; losses accumulate without recovery mechanisms
